channel.title: SSbusinessAndMarket
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SSbusinessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Analysts expected oil to surge above $200 but China has kept prices half that, and can't for much longer
item.description: Despite the Strait of Hormuz closure, oil prices have remained stable, JPMorgan analysts noted, but China's ability to keep them low is limited.

item.title: How the World Cup is a high-stakes stage for Big Tech's AI push
item.description: The World Cup, starting June 11, offers major tech firms like Lenovo, Google, and Salesforce a platform to showcase their AI advancements.

item.title: Honda recalls nearly 900,000 cars due to rear suspension issues
item.description: Faulty components could cause loss of control, leading to serious injury or crashes. Honda estimates only 1% of vehicles are affected.

item.title: Anthropic accused of secret sabotage as Claude Fable 5 silently limits capabilities for AI researchers and developers
item.description: A paragraph in Fable 5's 319-page system card revealed the model would quietly downgrade its responses for certain AI development work without notice.
